1.verse_text: Dark-complexioned Lord Krsna, whose form is eternal, blissful, and full of knowledge, formerly danced with the golden-complexioned gopis in the land of Vrndavana. By intently embracing them He attained a golden complexion like theirs. In this golden form He has now appeared in the town of Navadvipa.

clues.clue: The transcendental land where the dark complexioned Lord eternally danced with the golden gopis of Vraja
The specific town where the Lord appeared in His golden form to manifest His intimate pastimes

2.verse_text: We offer respectful obeisances to Lord Advaita and the other dear associates of the Lord, who are all full of compassion for the fallen souls. We offer our respectful obeisances to Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami and the other companions of the Lord. As the Lord Himself is full of auspicious transcendental qualities, so are they. As the Lord Himself is full of mercy, so are they. These great devotees are filled with the sweet nectar of Krsna-consciousness. These great devotees remove the grave sins of this material world.

clues.clue: The merciful companion of the Lord who is honored as a dear associate full of compassion
The intimate and learned personal companion of the Lord recognized for his great devotional nectar

3.verse_text: We offer our respectful obeisances to Srinatha Cakravarti, our spiritual master, who is very dear to the all-powerful Personality of Godhead. Our spiritual master is like a jewel decorating the earth, or like a splendid moon shining in the brahminical community. Some rare elevated souls in this world are able to understand and relish his nectarean description of Lord Krsna's confidential pastimes in Vrndavana.

clues.clue: The spiritual master of the author who shines like a splendid moon in the brahminical community.
The eternal abode where Lord Krsna performs His confidential and nectarean pastimes relishable by elevated souls.

4.verse_text: With great devotion, I offer my respectful obeisances to my father, Sri Sivanandana, who is like a brilliant lamp shining in the Sena family, and who is counted among the foremost associates of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The brilliant lamp of the Sena family and father of the author Kavi Karnapura

5.verse_text: Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami and other great Vaisnava authors have written in their books many descriptions of the great associates of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord Nityananda, and Lord Advaita. These authors have explained that the associates of Lord Caitanya had previously been cowherd people in Vrndavana during Lord Krsna's advent, and these authors have informed us of the former names of each of these associates of the Lord. I have carefully considered all these writings, and I have also heard the testimonies of the devotees in Bengal, Orissa, and Mathura. Collecting all this evidence, I, Paramananda dasa, write this book describing the previous life of the associates of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The eminent Vaishnava author whose writings provide the scriptural foundation for this study of eternal identities
The humble compiler and author who gathered testimonies from Bengal Orissa and Mathura to write this text
The eternal forest where the associates of Lord Caitanya performed their original pastimes as cowherd people

6.verse_text: Just as Lord Krsnacandra, who is non-different from the Panca-tattva, formerly appeared in this world, so Lord Caitanya also appeared in this world.

clues.clue: The moonlike Lord of Vraja who appeared in this world as the non-different Panca-tattva
The golden avatar who appeared in this world manifesting the non-different nature of the Panca-tattva

7.verse_text: The members of the Panca-tattva are non-different from Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Had they been different from Him, they would have been known as 'catstayam tattvam (the four truths)'.

clues.clue: The group of five spiritual principles that are accepted as non-different from the golden Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu
The Supreme Lord whose golden form in Navadvipa remains eternally identical with the collective members of the Panca-tattva

8.verse_text: The members of the Pance-tattva are different from Lord Krsna, and then again they are not different from Him. By employing His internal potency, the Lord has created this condition in which the members of the Panca-tattva are simultaneously different from Him and not different from Him.

clues.clue: The group of five associates who are simultaneously one with and different from Lord Krsna

9.verse_text: Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has described the five members of the Panca-tattva in the following words.

clues.clue: The intimate associate of Mahaprabhu who authoritatively outlined the five members of the Panca-tattva for all devotees.

10.verse_text: 'I offer my obeisances unto the Supreme Lord Krsna, who is non-different from His features as a devotee, devotional incarnation, devotional manifestation, pure devotee, and devotional energy.'

clues.clue: The dark complexioned source of all pleasure who appeared in Navadvipa as the golden Lord Gauranga

11.verse_text: I shall now summarily explain the meaning of these words. In this Panca-tattva, the bhakta-rupa (form of a devotee) is Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who formerly appeared as Lord Krsna, the son of Nanda Maharaja. The bhakta-svarupa (devotional incarnation) is Lord Nityananda, who formerly appeared in Vrajabhumi as Lord Balarama. The bhaktavatara (devotional manifestation) is Lord Advaita Acarya, who is not different from Lord Sadasiva. The bhaktakhya (pure devotee) is Srinivasa and the other great devotees as well. The bhakta-sakti (devotional energy) is Gadadhara Pandita, the foremost of brahmanas.

clues.clue: The golden Lord who is the bhakta rupa and formerly appeared as the son of Nanda Maharaja.
The bhakta svarupa of the Panca tattva who formerly appeared in Vrajabhumi as the mighty Lord Balarama.
The bhaktavatara who is recognized by the wise as being non different from the form of Sadasiva.
The foremost of the brahmanas identified as the bhakta sakti within the glorious Panca tattva assembly.

12.verse_text: Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda Avadhuta, and Lord Advaita, are all incarnations of the supremely exalted Personality of Godhead, and They are all known by the title Prabhu (Master). Among Them, Lord Caitanya, who is an ocean of mercy, is known as Mahaprabhu (The Great Master), and the great personalities Lord Nityananda and Lord Advaita are known only as Prabhu (Master). All three are also known as Gosvami (Master of the Senses). Gadadhara is called by the title Dvija (Brahmana), and Srinivasa is called by the title Pandita (Learned Scholar). These are the titles of the members of the Panca-tattva.

clues.clue: The unique title reserved exclusively for the ocean of mercy who is the primary member of the Panca-tattva.
A title of spiritual authority and sense control attributed to the three primary members of the Panca-tattva.
The specific designation for Gadadhara denoting his identity as a brahmana within the holy Panca-tattva group.
The honorable title of the learned scholar Srinivasa as recognized among the members of the Panca-tattva.

13.verse_text: In this regard Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has explained that the title Mahaprabhu should be used only for Lord Caitanya, although the title Prabhu may be used for Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda, and Lord Advaita. For this reason the devotees address Lord Caitanya as Mahaprabhu, and Lord Nityananda and Lord Advaita as Prabhu.

clues.clue: The learned associate who explicitly clarified the specific usage of the titles Mahaprabhu and Prabhu for devotees

14.verse_text: All the associates of Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda, and Lord Advaita are very exalted personalities. The association of Lord Nityananda were all cowherd boys during Lord Krsna's advent.

clues.clue: The personality whose eternal associates were all cowherd boys during the original Vraja lila of Lord Krsna
One of the three exalted personalities whose followers are described as being of the highest spiritual status

15.verse_text: The associates of Lord Caitanya at Navadvipa are considered the most exalted.

clues.clue: The holy island city where the most exalted associates of Lord Caitanya eternally reside and serve

16.verse_text: The next most exalted are the Lord's associates at Jagannath Puri, and after them are the great souls who met Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu in His journeys to South India and other places.

clues.clue: The holy coastal abode where the Lord resided and gathered His most exalted associates after Navadvipa

17.verse_text: In his description of Lord Caitanya, Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has explained that among the exalted associates of the Panca-tattva are devotees that were formerly cowherd boys who performed pastimes with Lord Krsna.

clues.clue: The intimate secretary of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu who authoritatively identified the eternal identities of the Gaura-lila associates
The group of five exalted manifestations of the Supreme Lord whose associates included former cowherd boys from Vraja

18.verse_text: Some learned devotees say that Navadvipa is identical with the land of Vrndavana. Others say that Navadvipa is actually the spiritual planet Goloka. Some say that Navadvipa is the planet Svetadvipa, and others say that Navadvipa is actually the spiritual sky of Vaikuntha. All glories to the wonderfully splendid land of Navadvipa.

clues.clue: The wonderfully splendid land praised for being eternally identical to the forests of Vrndavana and Goloka
The sacred forest of the Supreme Personality of Godhead that is eternally manifested within the land Navadvipa

19.verse_text: Taking the name Visvambhara, the Supreme Personality of Godhead accepted residence in the town of Navadvipa. He enjoyed many transcendental pastimes there with His exalted associates, and those pastimes have drowned the minds of this universe's residents in great transcendental bliss.

clues.clue: The name accepted by the Supreme Personality of Godhead when residing in the holy town of Navadvipa
The transcendental town where the Supreme Personality of Godhead enjoyed pastimes drowning the universe in bliss

20.verse_text: The same Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in Satya-yuga as the white-complexioned Lord Sukla, the same Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Treta-yuga as the red-complexioned Lord Makhamuk, the same Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Dvapara-yuga as the dark-complexioned Lord Syamasundara, has appeared in the Kali-yuga as the golden moon of Lord Caitanya, and engaged in the chanting of the holy names, the incarnation of Lord Hari.

clues.clue: The white complexioned Personality of Godhead who descended to the earth in the age of Satya yuga
The red complexioned incarnation of the Supreme Lord who appeared to perform pastimes in the Treta yuga
The dark complexioned eternal form of the Supreme Lord who manifested His pastimes in the Dvapara yuga
The golden moon who appeared in the current age of Kali to initiate the chanting of names

21.verse_text: In the Kali-yuga there are four Vaisnava sampradayas, known as the Sri, Brahma, Rudra, and Sanaka sampradaya. This is described in the following statement of the Padma Purana: 'In the Kali-yuga four Vaisnava sampradayas, the Sri, Brahma, Rudra, and Sanaka smapradayas, will purify the earth.'

clues.clue: The four sampradayas in Kali-yuga include the Sri, Sanaka, Rudra, and this lineage descending from the cosmic creator
Along with Sri, Brahma, and Rudra, this is one of the four essential Vaisnava sampradayas purifying the earth

22.verse_text: I shall now begin this book by describing the disciplic succesion descended from Sripada Madhvacarya. Lord Brahma, the creator of the universe became the disciple of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Narayana. Brahma's disciple was Narada. Narada's disciple was Vyasa. Vyasa then transmitted transcendental knowledge to his disciple Sukadeva. Sukadeva taught the same knowledge to his many disciples and grand-disciples in this world. The famous Madhvacarya received initiation from Vyasa personally. Madhvacaya carefully studied all the Vedas from Vyasa, and later wrote his book Mayavada-sata-dusani, where he proved that the Absolute Truth is the Supreme Person, full of all transcendental qualities, and not the qualityless impersonal Brahman. Madhvacarya's disciple was the exalted Padmanabhacarya. Padmanabhacarya's disciple was Narahari. Narahari's disciple was Madhava Dvija. Madhava Dvija's disciple was Aksobhya. Aksobhya's disciple was Jayatirtha. Jayatirtha's disciple was Jnanasindhu. Jnanasindhu's disciple was Mahanidhi. Mahanidhi's disciple was Vidyanidhi. Vidyanidhi's disciple was Rajendra. Rajendra's disciple was Jayadharma Muni. Among Jayadharma Muni's disciples was Sriman Visnupuri, the famous author of the Bhakti-ratnavali. Another disciple of Jayadharma was Brahmana Purusottama. Purusottama's disciple was Vyasatirtha, who wrote the famous book Sri Visnu-samhita. Vyasatirtha's disciple was Sriman Laksmipati, who was like a great reservoir of the nectar of devotional service. Laksmipati's disciple was Madhavendra Puri, a great preacher of devotional service. Madhavendra Puri was the incarnation of a kalpa-vrksa tree in the abode of Vraja. This tree bears as its fruits the mellows of servitude to Lord Krsna, friendship with Lord Krsna, parental love for Lord Krsna, and conjugal love for Lord Krsna.

clues.clue: The exalted teacher who received personal initiation from Vyasa and established the disciplic succession of Brahma
The direct and exalted disciple who carried the lamp of knowledge after the great teacher Madhvacarya
The incarnation of a kalpa-vrksa tree in Vraja who distributed the nectarous fruits of transcendental mellows

23.verse_text: Madhavendra Puri's disciple was Sriman Isvara Puri Svami. Isvara Puri carefully understood the mellows of conjugal love for Lord Krsna, and was able to distribute that fruit to others.

clues.clue: The devoted disciple of Madhavendra Puri who tasted and distributed the nectar of conjugal love for Krishna

24.verse_text: Sri Advaita Acarya displayed the sentiments of servitorship and friendship for the Lord, and Sriman Ranga Puri manifested the sentiment of parental love for Lord Krsna.

clues.clue: This associate of the Lord displayed the sentiments of servitorship and friendship toward the Supreme Personality of Godhead
The personality who manifested the sentiment of parental love for Lord Krsna during the pastimes of the Lord

25.verse_text: Lord Caitanya accepted Sriman Isvara Puri as His spiritual master. The Lord proceeded to flood the entire world with spontaneous transcendental love for Krsna.

clues.clue: The esteemed spiritual master accepted by Lord Caitanya to ignite the flood of spontaneous transcendental love
The golden Lord who initiated a global outpouring of divine affection after accepting His spiritual master

26.verse_text: Lord Caitanya accepted Srimati Radharani's golden complexion and ecstatic love for Lord Krsna, both of which had never before been seen in this world. Lord Caitanya was actually Lord Krsna, the son of Maharaja Nanda. He appeared like a great ocean of nectar flooding the entire world.

clues.clue: The king of Vraja whose son appeared in the world as the ocean of nectar Lord Caitanya

27.verse_text: When Lord Vasudeva, the first member of the Catur-vyuha, saw the dancing of the Gandharvas at Dvaraka Puri, His mind became agitated with the desire to dance as they did. In order to fulfill this desire, Lord Vasudeva entered the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya, to participate in the Lord's pastimes of ecstatic dancing.

clues.clue: The first member of the Caturvyuha who felt an intense desire to dance during the pastimes in Dvaraka
The opulent city where the Lord heard the Gandharvas and became inspired to dance in transcendental ecstasy

28.verse_text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead who remains at Dvaraka also entered the body of Lord Caitanya, the son of Saci-devi, and various other incarnations of the Supreme Lord also entered Lord Caitanya's body, all of them present there at the same time.

clues.clue: The chaste mother of Lord Caitanya whose name identifies the divine son in the main verse
The transcendental city from where the Supreme Personality of Godhead entered the body of Lord Caitanya

29.verse_text: All these incarnations entered the body of Lord Caitanya, just as the black incarnation Lord Syama formerly entered the transcendental body of Lord Krsna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

clues.clue: The black complexioned incarnation of the Lord who formerly entered the transcendental body of Krishna
The golden Lord in whose transcendental body all other divine incarnations gathered and resided simultaneously

30.verse_text: Although the various incarnations of the Lord, who all manifested inconceivable transcendental qualities, were situated in various parts of the spiritual and material worlds, nevertheless, by the power of the Lord's yogamaya energy, They all assembled together within the transcendental form of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The golden ocean of mercy who miraculously gathered all diverse incarnations within His own transcendental divine form
The unified form of all avatars whose yogamaya energy orchestrated the assembly of divine personalities into Himself

31.verse_text: These inconceivable workings of the yogamaya potency cannot be understood by the limited conceptual power of the conditioned soul. This is confirmed by the following statement of Srila Vyasadeva in the Prabhasa-khanda of the Mahabharata: 'The activities of the Personality of Godhead are inconceivable. Ordinary material logic does not have the strength to understand them.'

clues.clue: The great sage who speaks in the Prabhasa-khanda to confirm the inconceivable activities of the Supreme Lord.
The expansive epic work which contains the Prabhasa-khanda describing the nature of the Supreme Lord's transcendental activities.

32.verse_text: The various incarnations entered the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya just as formerly Lord Parasurama entered the body of Lord Ramacandra. Narada and other eternal devotees of the Lord also followed Him into this world to perform pastimes with Him in various places, just as the personified Vedas also did.

clues.clue: The incarnation who once entered the body of Lord Ramacandra and similarly entered Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu
The eternal sage and devotee who descended into this world alongside Lord Caitanya to perform pastimes

33.verse_text: According to their individual sentiments of ecstatic love for Lord Krsna, the various eternal devotees of the Lord assumed various roles in the Lord's pastimes in Vraja.

clues.clue: The eternal transcendental realm where the devotees assume roles in the Lord's intimate and ecstatic pastimes

34.verse_text: Just as the moon of Lord Caitanya was about to rise in this material world, the Lord said to Advaita Acarya: 'Some devotees are attached to Me in the spirit of servitude, some are attached to Me in the spirit of friendship, and others are attached to Me with a mixture of the spirits of servitude and friendship. Some have firm faith and love for My forms as Sri Sri Radha-Madhava, and others are attached to My form as the king of Dvaraka. Some are attached to My various incarnations and thus display various kinds of love for me. I shall show them how to love Me as the residents of Vraja did.'

clues.clue: The intimate associate whom Lord Caitanya instructed regarding the varying spiritual sentiments of His diverse loving devotees

35.verse_text: The cowherd man named Parjanya who was Krsna's paternal grandfather in Vrndavana, took birth in the town of Sri Hatta during Lord Caitanya's pastimes. Parjanya took the name Upendra Misra and became the father of seven sons.

clues.clue: The cowherd grandfather of Krishna in Vrndavana who took the name Upendra Misra in Sri Hatta
The father of seven sons who is the eternal form of the cowherd man Parjanya

36.verse_text: The gopi named Variyasi-devi, who was Lord Krsna's paternal grandmother in Vraja, appeared as Kamalavati-devi during Lord Caitanya's advent.

clues.clue: The grandmother of Lord Krsna in Vraja who manifested as Kamalavati during the advent of Lord Caitanya
The earthly identity taken by the gopi Variyasi during the descent of the golden Lord in Navadvipa

37.verse_text: Srimati Yasoda-devi and Vraja's king Nanda, who had been like two great oceans of the nectar of love for Krsna during the Lord's pastimes in Vrndavana, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srimati Saci-devi and Sriman Jagannatha Purandara. Of this there is no doubt.

clues.clue: The golden Lord Gauranga's mother who was previously the great ocean of love named Yasoda in Vrndavana

38-39.verse_text: Some devotees say that the two demigods Aditi and Kasyapa, who incarnated as Kausalya and Dasaratha, Prsni and Sutapa, and also as Devaki and Vasudeva, the parents of Balarama and Krsna, also entered the bodies of Saci-devi and Jagannatha. These devotees say that if this were not so, then it would not have been possible for Balarama to take birth as their son Visvarupa.

clues.clue: The elder brother of Lord Caitanya who manifested as the birth son of Saci and Jagannatha
The mother of Lord Ramacandra who is identified as one of the past forms of Saci-devi
The glorious king of Ayodhya whose identity merged into the father of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu

40.verse_text: Rohini and Vasudeva, the parents of Balarama and Krsna, appeared during Lord Caitanya's advent as the exalted brahmana couple Padmavati and Mukunda. Sumitra and Dasaratha were also present in the bodies of Padmavati and Mukunda.

clues.clue: The exalted brahmana mother of Nimai who was the earthly incarnation of Rohini and Sumitra combined
The father of the golden Lord who appeared in the pastimes of Caitanya as Dasaratha himself

41.verse_text: The same Paurnamasi who greatly pleased Lord Govinda in Vraja appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srila Govinda Acarya, the expert author of songs and poetry.

clues.clue: The internal potency of the Lord who performed the role of yoga maya in the pastimes of Vraja
The expert composer of poetry who appeared in the Gaura-lila as an incarnation of the divine Paurnamasi

42.verse_text: Ambika, the nurse who fed infant Krsna with her own breast milk in Vraja, appeared during Lord Caitany's pastimes as Malini-devi, the wife of Srivasa Pandita.

clues.clue: The devoted wife of Srivasa Pandita who serves as the incarnation of the Vraja nurse Ambika
The husband of Malini-devi who acts as the primary shelter for the Lord's pastimes in Navadvipa

43.verse_text: Kilambika-devi, who had been Ambika's sister in Vraja, and who had been accustomed to eating the remnants of foodstuff eaten by Lord Krsna, appeared during Lord Caitayna's pastimes as Narayani-devi.

clues.clue: The sister of Ambika in Vraja who relished Krsna's food remnants and appeared in Gaura-lila as this devotee.
The Vraja-lila counterpart to Narayani-devi who was accustomed to consuming the food remnants of Lord Krsna.

44.verse_text: The same person who was Maharaja Janaka, the king of Mithila, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vallabhacarya. Some people differ from this view, and say that Vallabhacarya was an incarnation of Maharaja Bhismaka.

clues.clue: The king of Mithila in ancient times who descended as a prominent scholar during Lord Caitanyas manifest pastimes
The personality who is historically identified as the incarnation of either Maharaja Janaka or the king Maharaja Bhismaka
The ruler of Vidarbha whose identity some devotees believe was manifested through the appearance of the scholar Vallabhacarya

45.verse_text: The same person who formerly appeared as Janaki-devi and Rukmini-devi appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srimati Laksmipriya, the daughter of Vallabhacarya.

clues.clue: The daughter of Vallabhacarya who eternally embodies the grace and divine presence of both Janaki and Rukmini
The noble father whose blessed daughter is identified as the earthly manifestation of the goddess of fortune

46.verse_text: Vallbhacarya's daughter, Srimati Laksmipriya, was an incarnation of Srimati Laksmi-devi, the goddess of fortune. One day, as she was walking with her friends towards the bank of the Ganges to bathe, she entered the path of Lord Caitanya's vision. In this way the Lord saw Laksmipriya for the first time.

clues.clue: The daughter of Vallabhacarya and an incarnation of the goddess of fortune seen by Lord Caitanya.
The father whose daughter was an incarnation of the goddess of fortune in the pastimes of Gauranga.

47.verse_text: The same person who formerly appeared as King Satrajit appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sanatana Misra. Sanatana Misra's daughter was Srimati Visnupriya, and she was the incarnation of the Lord's Bhu-sakti, who is the mother of the entire universe.

clues.clue: The king who formerly appeared in the pastimes of the Lord and returned as Sanatana Misra
The beloved daughter of Sanatana Misra who acts as the incarnation of the universal mother Bhu
The noble father who is identified as the earthly manifestation of the ancient King Satrajit

48.verse_text: That Srimati Visnupriya is an incarnation of Bhu-devi is described in the Sri Caitanya-candrodaya-nataka, where Kali says: 'Understanding that Srimati Visnupriya was the incarnation of Bhu-devi, Lord Caitanya accepted her hand in marriage.'

clues.clue: The golden Lord Gauranga accepted the hand of this person who is known as the incarnation of Bhudevi.

49.verse_text: The same person who appeared as Visvamitra Muni, who arranged the marriage of Lord Ramacandra, and who also appeared as the brahmana messenger sent by Srimati Rukmini-devi to Lord Kesava, also appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vanamali Acarya, who was famous as a learned astrologer.

clues.clue: The learned astrologer in Caitanya lila who previously served as the messenger for Rukmini to Lord Kesava
The great sage who arranged the marriage of Ramacandra and later appeared in the pastimes of Gaura

50.verse_text: The brahmana named Kulaka, whom Maharaja Satrajit sent to Lord Madhava to arrange the Lord's marriage with Satyabhama, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Kasinatha.

clues.clue: The brahmana messenger sent by Maharaja Satrajit to arrange a marriage with the lotus-eyed Lord Madhava
The devoted associate of Lord Caitanya who previously acted as the messenger for Maharaja Satrajit
The beloved queen of Lord Madhava whose marriage was arranged through the messenger named Kulaka

51.verse_text: The person who appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Jagadananda Pandita was actually an incarnation of Srimati Satyabhama-devi. What devotee can say otherwise?

clues.clue: The intimate associate of Lord Caitanya who serves as the earthly incarnation of Srimati Satyabhama devi
The exalted queen of Dvaraka who assumed the identity of an intimate pandita in Lord Caitanya's pastimes

52.verse_text: The same person who appeared as Sandipani Muni, the sage who gave Lord Krsna the sacred thread in Mathura, has appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Kesava Bharati.

clues.clue: The sage who initiated Lord Krsna with the sacred thread in Mathura and appeared as Kesava Bharati
The sannyasa guru of Lord Caitanya who served as the earthly manifestation of the sage Sandipani Muni

53.verse_text: The same person who formerly appeared as Vasistha Muni, the spiritual master of Lord Ramacandra, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Gangadasa and Sudarsana.

clues.clue: The venerable sage of the Ramayana who served as the spiritual master of Lord Ramacandra
The personality in Gaura-lila who historically appeared as the spiritual master of Lord Ramacandra
A manifestation of the spiritual master of Lord Ramacandra appearing within the pastimes of Caitanya

54.verse_text: The same person who was formerly known as Maharaja Vrsabhanu in the district of Vraja, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the great personality Pundarika Vidyanidhi.

clues.clue: The king of Vraja who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as the great soul Pundarika Vidyanidhi.

55.verse_text: When He was afflicted by the feelings of Srimati Radharani's intense love in separation from Lord Krsna, Lord Caitanya would address Pundarika Vidyanidhi using the word 'father'.

clues.clue: The king of Vraja whose earthly identity was assumed by Pundarika Vidyanidhi during the pastimes of Gaura

56-57.verse_text: Lord Caitanya was very pleased with Pundarika Vidyanidhi, and the Lord would address him by the name Premanidhi. Because Pundarika Vidyanidhi was a direct disciple of Madhavendra Puri, the Lord would treat him with great respect, as if he were the Lord's own spiritual master. Maharaja Vrsabhanu also appeared as Sri Madhava Misra. Ratnavati-devi, Pundarika Vidyanidhi's wife, is considered by the learned devotees to be the incarnation of Kirtida-devi, the wife of Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

clues.clue: The intimate name used by Lord Caitanya to address the highly respected devotee Pundarika Vidyanidhi.
The devotee who appeared in the pastimes of Gaura as the incarnation of King Vrsabhanu.
The wife of Pundarika Vidyanidhi who is recognized by the learned as the incarnation of Kirtida-devi.

58.verse_text: The original form of the Supreme Lord is considered not different from the Lord's direct expansions. For this reason it is considered that Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, the son of Srimati Saci-devi, is non-different from His expansion Lord Vasudeva, the first member of the Catur-vyuha. In the same way Sri Visvarupa is considered non-different from the incarnation Lord Sankarsana, the second member of the Catur-vyuha.

clues.clue: The devoted mother of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu who is the source of the Lord's appearance in Navadvipa.
The elder brother of Lord Caitanya who is considered non-different from the divine incarnation Lord Sankarsana.
The expansion of the Catur-vyuha who is eternally non-different from the golden form of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

59.verse_text: Lord Nityananda Avadhuta is also said to be non-different from Lord Sankarsana. That Sri Visvarupa is identical with Lord Sankarsana is described in the Sri Caitanya-candrodaya-nataka, where Kali says to Dharma the following words:

clues.clue: The divine Avadhuta who is understood to be non-different from the eternal expansion Lord Sankarsana
The second member of the Catur-vyuha with whom Lord Nityananda and Sri Visvarupa are identical
The elder brother of Lord Caitanya described as identical with the expansion of the Personality of Godhead

60.verse_text: 'Lord Caitanya's elder brother Visvarupa is identical with the Personality of Godhead Lord Sankarsana. Visvarupa never married, but accepted the order of parivrajakacarya-sannyasa and left home. It was the transcendental glory of Sri Visvarupa that caused Lord Caitanya to accept spiritual initiation from Isvara Puri.'

clues.clue: The elder brother of Lord Caitanya who renounced home to accept the order of parivrajakacarya sannyasa
The supreme personality of godhead identified as the eternal identity of the golden lord's elder brother
The holy spiritual master whose initiation was sought by Lord Caitanya due to the influence of Visvarupa

61.verse_text: That Lord Nityananda is also non-different from Lord Sankarsana is described in the following statement of the Sri Caitanya-candrodaya-nataka: 'Lord Nityananda Avadhuta is non-different from Lord Sankarsana.'

clues.clue: The personality described in the Caitanya-candrodaya-nataka as being non-different from the eternal form of Lord Sankarsana
The supreme expansion of the Lord whom the holy Avadhuta is revealed to be non-different from

62.verse_text: When the eternal Personality of Godhead Lord Visvarupa disappeared from home, He entered the body of Lord Nityananda Avadhuta.

clues.clue: The eternal Personality of Godhead who vanished from his home to enter the body of Nityananda
The exalted Avadhuta who received the presence of the disappeared Lord Visvarupa into his own transcendental form

63.verse_text: My father, Sivananda Sena, once said: 'Lord Nityananda Avadhuta is actually the Personality of Godhead Lord Balarama. Always staying among the devotees, He shines with the splendor of thousands of suns.' Saying this, he began to dance.

clues.clue: The devoted father of Kavi Karnapura who ecstaticly danced while glorifying the transcendental position of Lord Nityananda
The original personality of Godhead whom the author's father identified as being identical to Lord Nityananda Avadhuta

64.verse_text: Lord Nityananda incarnates as His plenary expansion Ananta Sesa. As Lord Sesa He transforms His body to become the garments, bracelets, and other ornaments worn by Lord Visnu and Lord Krsna. Expanding again as the pastime-potency, lila-sakti, Lord Sesa is aware of all the details of Lord Krsna's confidential pastimes.

clues.clue: The expansive plenary form who transforms Himself into the garments and ornaments adorning the Supreme Lord
The transcendental serpentine personality aware of all the confidential details of the pastimes of Lord Krsna

65.verse_text: Lord Balarama's two wives, Srimati Varuni-devi and Srimati Revati-devi, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srimati Vasudha-devi and Srimati Jahnavi-devi, the two dear wives of Lord Nityananda, and the daughters of the great personality Sri Surya-dasa. Their father, Surya-dasa, had previously been Maharaja Kakudmi. His bodily luster made him as effulgent as the sun.

clues.clue: One of the two dear wives of Lord Nityananda who appeared as an incarnation of Varunidevi
The devoted wife of Lord Nityananda recognized as the eternal expansion of Revatidevi in Gauranga lila
The effulgent father of Vasudha and Jahnavi who previously served as the king known as Kakudmi

66.verse_text: Some say that Srimati Vasudha-devi is the incarnation of Srimati Ananga-manjari, and others say that Srimati Jahnavi-devi is the incarnation of Srimati Ananga-manjari. In truth, both opinions are correct. They are both incarnations of Srimati Ananga-manjari.

clues.clue: The wife of Lord Nityananda who is recognized by many as an expansion of Srimati Ananga-manjari
The venerable wife of Lord Nityananda who also shares the identity of being Srimati Ananga-manjari
The eternal gopi whose divine manifestation is shared between the two wives of Lord Nityananda

67.verse_text: The expansion of Lord Sankarsana known as Lord Ksirodakasayi Visnu appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Viracandra Prabhu. Lord Viracandra was non-different from Lord Caitanya Himself.

clues.clue: The expansion of Lord Ksirodakasayi Visnu who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu

68.verse_text: Lord Viracandra also entered the bodies of the two brothers Nisatha and Ulmuka, who in this way were incarnations of Lord Visnu. Minaketana Rama dasa and others were also incarnations of Lord Sankarsana in this way.

clues.clue: The incarnation of Lord Ksirodakasayi Visnu who entered the bodies of the two brothers Nisatha and Ulmuka
One of the two brothers whose bodies were entered by Lord Viracandra as an incarnation of Visnu
One of the two brothers who served as an incarnation of Lord Visnu by Lord Viracandra's entry
The devotee identified in the text as being an incarnation of Lord Sankarsana in Caitanya lila

69.verse_text: The Ganges River, who was born from the lotus feet of Lord Visnu, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srimati Ganga-devi, the daughter of Lord Nityananda. Her husband, Sri Madhava, had formerly been Maharaja Santanu.

clues.clue: The sacred river flowing from the feet of Lord Visnu who appeared as the daughter of Nityananda
The husband of Gangadevi who previously held the identity of the Kuru dynasty king named Santanu

70.verse_text: Lord Pradyumna, the third member of the Catur-vyuha, had formerly appeared in Vrajabhumi as a close (priya-narma-sakha) friend of Sri Sri Radha-Madhava, where he helped in Their transcendental pastimes. That same Lord Pradyumna appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Raghunananda Thakura, who was non-different from both Lord Caitanya and Lord Advaita.

clues.clue: The third member of the Caturvyuha who served as a priya-narma-sakha to Radha and Madhava in Vraja

71.verse_text: Lord Aniruddha, the fourth member of the Catur-vyuha, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vakresvara Pandita. Lord Krsna personally entered Vakresvara's body and caused Him to dance in ecstasy. This ecstatic dancing of Vakresvara Pandita delights Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The fourth member of the Caturvyuha who danced in ecstasy as Lord Krsna personally entered his body
The identity of the fourth expansion of the Caturvyuha who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya

72.verse_text: One day Vakresvara Pandita sweetly said to Lord Caitanya: 'O merciful Lord, please give me a thousand Gandharva singers to sing as I dance.'

clues.clue: The ecstatic dancer who requested one thousand Gandharva singers to accompany his performance for the Lord.

73.verse_text: A particle of Lord Caitanya entered the body of Nakula Brahmacari. In this way Nakula Brahmacari became a saktyavesa incarnation of the Lord.

clues.clue: The saintly devotee who became a saktyavesa incarnation after receiving a particle of Lord Caitanyas energy

74.verse_text: In the same way, a particle of Lord Caitanya also entered the body of Pradyumna Misra. Bhagavan Acarya Khanja is said to be a partial manifestation of Lord Gaura.

clues.clue: The faithful associate whose form was entered by a particle of Lord Caitanya to manifest His divine potency

75.verse_text: Lord Brahma, the master of the universe, who is considered one of the members of the Nava-vyuha and who is the original teacher of the Vedas and Tantras, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Gopinatha Acarya.

clues.clue: The master of the universe and original teacher of Vedas who entered the pastimes of Lord Caitanya
The learned devotee in Sri Caitanya's pastimes who is identified as the incarnation of Lord Brahma

76.verse_text: Lord Sadasiva, who formerly appeared as an avesa-incarnation in Vrajabhumi, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Lord Advaita Gosvami, who is non-different from Lord Caitanya Himself.

clues.clue: The eternal expansion of Sadasiva who manifested within the pastimes of Lord Caitanya in Vrajabhumi
The Lord who danced with Krishna as a cowherd boy and later appeared as Advaita Gosvami

77.verse_text: Assuming the form of a cowherd boy, Lord Sadasiva danced with Lord Krsna in Vrajabhumi. This is confirmed by the following statement of the Siva-tantra, where Bhairava says:

clues.clue: The eternal lord of the Himalayas who danced in the form of a cowherd boy in Vrajabhumi
The divine land where the supreme lord danced eternally with his intimate cowherd associates

78.verse_text: 'One day, during the great festival of lights in the month of Karttika, Lord Krsna enthusiastically danced with Lord Balarama and Their cowherd friends.'

clues.clue: The elder brother of Lord Krsna who enthusiastically participated in the festive dance of the cowherd boys
The sacred month characterized by the great festival of lights where the Lord danced with His associates

79.verse_text: 'My dear Parvati, when Lord Sadasiva, my spiritual master, saw this transcendental dancing, He yearned to become a cowherd boy and be able thus to dance with Lord Krsna.'

clues.clue: The spiritual master of Kavi Karnapura who desired to serve as a cowherd boy in Vraja
The consort of the Lord to whom Kavi Karnapura recounts the desire of the great spiritual master

80.verse_text: 'By Lord Krsna's mercy Lord Sadasiva was able to appear in two forms. In one form He remained as Lord Sadasiva, and in the other form He appeared as a cowherd boy in Vraja.'

clues.clue: The transcendental lord who appeared in two forms to participate in the pastimes of Vraja
The eternal abode where the cowherd boy form of Lord Sadasiva delights in divine dancing

81.verse_text: Lord Siva's friend Kuvera, the king of the Guhyakas, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Kuvera Pandita, the father of Lord Advaita.

clues.clue: The king of the Guhyakas and friend of Lord Siva who appeared as the father of Advaita Acarya.
The personality whose father in Gaura-lila was known as the king of the Guhyakas named Kuvera.

82.verse_text: Formerly, on Mount Kailasa, where the Siddhas and Sadhyas reside, Kuvera, who is very dear to Lord Siva, chanted the transcendental mantra containing the names of Lord Siva.

clues.clue: The king of the Guhyakas and friend of Lord Siva who chanted mantras on Mount Kailasa
The sacred mountain residence of the Siddhas where the friend of Lord Siva chanted the holy name

83.verse_text: Merciful Lord Siva then said to Kuvera: 'Please ask for some benediction. It is My desire to give you some gift.' Kuvera then asked for a benediction, saying, 'O my Lord, please become my son.'

clues.clue: The devoted treasurer of the demigods who requested Lord Siva to take birth as his own son
The merciful master of the demigods who agreed to appear in the material world as a son

84.verse_text: Requested in this way, Lord Sadasiva, the master of the demigods and the best of philanthropists, said to Kuvera: 'During my next birth in the material world I shall accept the position of being your son.'

clues.clue: The master of the demigods who promised the wealthy demigod Kuvera that he would become his son
The demigod who received the great fortune of having the Lord himself appear as his own son

85.verse_text: In this way Kuvera attained a very difficult to attain benediction. When the appropriate time arrived, by Lord Sadasiva's mercy, Kuvera became the father of Lord Advaita Acarya.

clues.clue: The exalted personality born as the son of Kuvera, recognized as the manifestation of Lord Sadasiva in Gaura-lila.
The pious figure who received a difficult benediction and became the earthly father of the great Advaita Acarya.
The master of the demigods who mercifully appeared within the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as Advaita Acarya.

86.verse_text: The Supreme Lord's yogamaya potency appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Sita-devi, the wife of Lord Advaita Acarya.

clues.clue: The manifestation of the Supreme Lord's yogamaya potency who served as the wife of Lord Advaita Acarya
The venerable devotee whose wife is identified as the Lord's internal potency in the pastimes of Gauranga

87.verse_text: Lord Advaita's son was Acyutananda, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya. Acyutananda was the dear disciple of Sriman Pandita Gosvami. This I have heard from the devotees.

clues.clue: The son of Advaita Acarya and a very dear associate who attained the mercy of Lord Caitanya
The learned disciple of Sriman who acted as the spiritual guide for Acyutananda in the Lord's pastimes

88.verse_text: Some say that Acyutananda was the incarnation of Karttikeya, and others learned in transcendental mellows say that he is the incarnation of Acyuta-gopi. Both views are correct, for both Karttikeya and Acyuta-gopi were present in the body of Acyutananda. Aside from this, some devotees say that Sri Krsna Misra was also the incarnation of Karttikeya.

clues.clue: The son of Lord Advaita, who manifested both the energy of Karttikeya and the Vraja-gopi known as Acyuta
An associate of the Lord who is considered by some devotees to be an incarnation of Karttikeya

89.verse_text: The devotees Nandini and Jangali who were associates of Lord Caitanya had previously been Jaya and Vijaya in Lord Krsna's pastimes.

clues.clue: An associate of Lord Caitanya who served as Jaya during the eternal pastimes of Lord Krsna
A devotee of Lord Caitanya who eternally functions as the expansion of Vijaya from Krsna lila

90.verse_text: Intelligent Srivasa Pandita had previously been Narada Muni, the best of the sages. Srivasa's younger brother, Sriman Rama Pandita, had previously been Narada's close friend Parvata Muni.

clues.clue: The intelligent associate of Lord Caitanya who, in Krishna lila, was known as the great sage Narada Muni.
The younger brother of Srivasa who appeared in the pastimes of Gaura as the friend of Narada Muni.
The intimate friend of Narada Muni who manifested in the age of Kali as Sriman Rama Pandita.

91.verse_text: The devotee named Murari Gupta in Lord Caitanya's pastimes had previously been Hanuman. In the same way Sri Purandara had previously been Angadam and Govindananda had been Sugriva.

clues.clue: The devoted associate of Lord Caitanya who eternally serves as the monkey warrior Hanuman
The intimate follower of the Lord who manifested in Ramacandra's pastimes as the powerful Angada
The personality in Gaura-lila who historically functioned as the monkey king Sugriva in the Ramayana

92.verse_text: The devotee named Ramacandra Puri in Lord Caitanya's pastimes had previously been Vibhisana.

clues.clue: The devotee who acted as the spiritual critic of Lord Caitanya and previously appeared as the brother Vibhisana
The faithful brother of Ravana who appeared in the pastimes of the golden Lord as Ramacandra Puri

93.verse_text: Lord Caitanya Himself did not accept that Ramacandra Puri was the incarnation of Vibhisana, but the Lord himself said that Ramacandra Puri was the empowered incarnation of Srimati Radharani's mother-in-law Jatila. This Ramacandra Puri restricted Lord Caitanya in His acceptance of alms. Rcika Muni's son Brahma Mahatapah, and Prahlada Maharaja combined to appear as Haridasa Thakur in Lord Caitanya's pastimes.

clues.clue: The mother in law of Srimati Radharani who appeared in Gaura lila as the personality Ramacandra Puri
The exalted devotee who appeared through the combined identity of Brahma Mahatapah and the great devotee Prahlada Maharaja

94-95.verse_text: In the book Caitanya-carita, Murari Gupta explains that one morning Mahatapah gave an unwashed tulasi leaf to his father, who became so angry at him for this that he cursed him. Because of this Mahatapah took birth in a yavana family as the great devotee Haridasa Thakura.

clues.clue: The author of Caitanya-carita who recounts the story of the curse placed upon the son Mahatapah.
The great soul who appeared in a yavana family after receiving a curse from his earthly father.

96-97.verse_text: The eight mystic powers, beginning with anima-siddhi, which had formerly appeared in Vrndavana, took birth in Bengal during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the eight devotees Ananta, Sukhananda, Govinda, Raghunatha, Krsnananda, Kesava, Damodara, and Raghava. All these devotees bore the title Puri after their names.

clues.clue: One of the eight mystics appearing in Bengal as a devotee holding the title Puri during Caitanya pastimes
A mystic power from Vraja taking the form of a Puri initiated devotee during the Gaura lila
One of the eight empowered devotees who left Vrndavana to perform pastimes with Lord Caitanya in Bengal

98-101.verse_text: The nine Jayanteyas, who were fixed in celibacy and equal vision, and who spoke the Sri Bhagavata-samhita to Janaka Rsi, appeared on this earth as nine sannyasis, and continually performed pastimes with Lord Caitanya. Their names were: Sri Nrsimhananda Tirtha, Sri Satyananda Bharati, Sri Nrsimha Tirtha, Sri Cidananda Tirtha, Sri Jagannatha Tirtha, Sri Vasudeva Tirtha, Sri Rama Tirtha, Sri Purusottama Tirtha, and Sri Garuda Avadhuta, who was also known as Sri Gopendra Asrama.

clues.clue: The nine pure brothers known for their celibacy and steady vision who appeared as nine ascetic sannyasis.
The distinct personality also known as Sri Garuda Avadhuta who performed eternal pastimes alongside the golden Lord.

102-103.verse_text: Kuvera's famous nine treasures, which include his conch and lotus, took birth during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi. Their names were Srinidhi, Srigarbha, Kaviratna, Sudhanidhi, Vidyanidhi, Gunanidhi, the learned brahmana Ratnabahu, Sriman Acaryaratna, and Sri Ratnakara Pandita.

clues.clue: One of the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi who manifest as the famous treasures of Lord Kuvera
The learned scholar who appears in Gaura-lila as one of the nine treasures of Lord Kuvera
The esteemed son of Nidhiratna-devi who reflects the fortune of a divine treasure in human form

104.verse_text: Before the assembled relatives and friends, Nilambara Cakravarti spoke the astrological prediction of Lord Caitanya's future life. For this reason it is said the Nilambara Cakravarti is the incarnation of Garga Muni.

clues.clue: The sage of Vraja who previously performed the name-giving ceremony and is now the maternal grandfather

105.verse_text: Srimati Saci-devi's father had formerly been the cowherd Sumukha, who was Yasoda-devi's father. Saci-devi's mother had formerly been Patala-devi, the wife of Sumukha in Vrajabhumi.

clues.clue: The father of Saci-devi who appeared previously in the eternal pastimes of Vrajabhumi as Yasoda-devi's father.
The mother of Saci-devi who formerly served as the wife of Sumukha within the sacred land of Vrajabhumi.

106.verse_text: Lord Caitanya's associate Sri Devananda Pandita, who was very learned in the Puranas, had previously been Bhaguri Muni, the chief pandita in the court of Maharaja Nanda.

clues.clue: The learned associate of Lord Caitanya who previously served as the chief pandita Bhaguri Muni
The esteemed sage who served in the court of Maharaja Nanda before becoming Devananda Pandita

107.verse_text: The four learned devotees in Lord Caitanya's pastimes, named Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha, were incarnations of the four Kumaras. Of this there is no doubt.

clues.clue: This associate of Lord Caitanya represents one of the four Kumaras and carries a name ending in natha
One of the four learned devotees who incarnated as a Kumara in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya
A scholar among the four Nathas who is famously known as an incarnation of a specific Kumara
The final devotee of the group of four who joined Lord Caitanya and reflects a Kumara identity

108.verse_text: The names of these four devotees each end with the word 'natha', just as the names of the four Kumaras each end with the word 'kumara'. These four devotees became known as the 'four Nathas'.

clues.clue: This devotee is one of the four learned figures serving Lord Caitanya and representing the four Kumaras
One of the four Nathas who serves as a manifestation of the Kumaras in the Gaura lila
The learned associate of Lord Caitanya whose name concludes with natha and honors the Kumaras
A member of the group known as the four Nathas who embodies the nature of a Kumara

109.verse_text: Pedavyasa appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vrndavana das Thakura. Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Kusumapida also entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura.

clues.clue: The cowherd friend of Lord Krsna who entered the body of the author of Caitanya Bhagavata

110.verse_text: Sukadeva Gosvami, the son of Srila Vyasadeva, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the devotee named Vallabha Bhatta.

clues.clue: The son of Srila Vyasadeva who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as Vallabha Bhatta
The devotee who revealed the identity of Sukadeva Gosvami during the pastimes of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu

111.verse_text: Sri Jagannatha Acarya, who was also known as Gangadasa Pandita, was very dear to Lord Caitanya. In his previous life he had been Durvasa Muni, who delighted the gopis and was very dear to them.

clues.clue: The dear associate of Lord Caitanya also known as Jagannatha Acarya who resided in eternal devotion
The sage of great power who in a previous life brought joy to the gopis of Vraja

112.verse_text: The learned devotees know that Candrasekhara Acarya was an incarnation of the moon-god and Sriman Uddhava dasa was an empowered incarnation of the moon-god.

clues.clue: The fortunate soul identified as an empowered incarnation who descends as a manifestation of the moon god

113.verse_text: Lord Caitanya personally affirmed that Candrasekhara Acarya was an incarnation of the moon-god, and in the same way the Lord expressly said that Sriman Visvesvara Acarya was an incarnation of the sun-god.

clues.clue: The personality explicitly identified by Lord Caitanya as an empowered incarnation of the brilliant sun-god

114.verse_text: Bhaskara Thakura was an incarnation of the demigod Visvakarma. Vanamali Tahkura was an incarnation of Sudama Brahmana, who was afflicted by great poverty and obtained great wealth from the Lord.

clues.clue: The devotee who appeared in the pastimes of the Lord as an incarnation of the demigod Visvakarma
The personality who represents the expansion of Sudama Brahmana who was once afflicted by great material poverty
The humble devotee whose past poverty was alleviated by receiving divine wealth directly from the Lord

115.verse_text: Jaya and Vijaya, the two doorkeepers of Vaikunthaloka, voluntarily appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the two devotees Sri Jagannatha and Sri Madhava.

clues.clue: The Vaikuntha doorkeeper who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya alongside his eternal companion
The second Vaikuntha gatekeeper who manifested in Gaura-lila as a dear devotee of the Lord
The devotee of Lord Caitanya who eternally serves as an incarnation of the Vaikuntha doorkeeper
The name of the associate who serves alongside Jagannatha as a manifestation of a Vaikuntha gatekeeper

116.verse_text: The two liberated souls Pundarikaksa and Kumuda, who are very famous in Vaikunthaloka, appeared in Bengal as the two devotees name Govinda and Garuda, who were both very dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: This famous liberated soul of Vaikunthaloka appeared in Bengal alongside Kumuda as a dear devotee of Lord Caitanya
The name taken by the liberated soul Pundarikaksa upon his appearance in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu

117.verse_text: Garuda Pandita was the incarnation of Garuda. Gopinatha Simha was the incarnation of Akrura. Some say that Kesava Bharati was the incarnation of Akrura.

clues.clue: This dear associate of Lord Caitanya is revealed to be an incarnation of the divine bird Garuda
This devotee is identified in the Gaura-ganoddesha-dipika as the earthly incarnation of Krishna's messenger Akrura
Some scholars affirm that this sannyasi, who initiated the Lord, is an incarnation of the devotee Akrura

118.verse_text: Sri Paramananda Puri had formerly been Uddhava. Maharaja Prataparudra, who was as powerful as Lord Indra, had formerly been Maharaja Indradyumna, who began the worship of Lord Jagannatha.

clues.clue: The intimate associate of Mahaprabhu who eternally serves as the Vraja devotee Uddhava in Krishna lila
The powerful king who was formerly the pious Maharaja Indradyumna and established the worship of Lord Jagannatha
The ancient monarch who began the service of Lord Jagannatha and later appeared as Maharaja Prataparudra

119.verse_text: Sarvabhauma Bhattacarya had formerly been Brhaspati, the foremost scholar in the heavenly planets.

clues.clue: The master of heavenly wisdom who descended to earth in the form of an exalted scholar

120.verse_text: The cowherd boy Arjuna who was a close friend of the Lord in Vrndavana, and the Pandava Arjuna, both joined to become Ramananda Raya, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The personality who combines the identities of the Pandava hero and the intimate cowherd friend of Krsna
The eternal cowherd boy of Vrndavana whose transcendental identity manifests within the beloved associate of Lord Caitanya

121.verse_text: The exalted personality, Ramanda Raya, for many days described to Lord Caitanya the true nature of pure love for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna.

clues.clue: The exalted devotee who revealed the deepest truths of pure love for Radha and Krishna to Mahaprabhu

122-124.verse_text: Some say that Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of Lalita-gopi, and others say that because Lord Caitanya directly told Ramananda Raya's father, Bhavananda Raya, that he was the incarnation of Maharaja Pandu, Arjuna's father, therefore Ramananda Raya must be the incarnation of Pandava Arjuna. The most learned devotees say that Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of both the Pandava Arjuna, and a gopi named Arjuniya. This explanation is also supported by the statements of the Padma Purana, Uttara-khanda. From all this we may conclude that Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of Lalita-gopi, Arjuniya-gopi, and Pandava Arjuna.

clues.clue: The intimate associate of Lord Caitanya described as an incarnation of Lalita Arjuniya and Pandava Arjuna
The father of Ramananda Raya who was identified by Lord Caitanya as an incarnation of Maharaja Pandu
The specific gopi whose identity is combined with the Pandava Arjuna in the personality of Ramananda Raya

125.verse_text: Now, as far as I am able, I shall describe the devotees who had previously appeared with the Lord in His Vraja-pastimes.

clues.clue: The transcendental abode where the eternal pastimes of the Lord originally take place before appearing in this world

126.verse_text: The cowherd boy named Sridama appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Ramadasa Abhirama dasa, who carried a flute made of a bamboo stick with sixteen knots.

clues.clue: The young Vrajavasi cowherd who descended into Gaura-lila as the flute-carrying devotee known as Ramadasa Abhirama
The prominent associate of Mahaprabhu who flourished as the manifestation of the cowherd boy Sridama from Vraja

127.verse_text: Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Sudama appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sundara Thakura. Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Vasudama appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Dhananjaya Pandita.

clues.clue: The manifestation of Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Sudama within the blissful pastimes of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu
The devoted soul who descended as the earthly identity of Lord Krsna's cowherd friend known as Vasudama

128.verse_text: Lord Krsna's dearest friend Subala appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Gauridasa Pandita. The cowherd boy Mahabala appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Kamalakara Pippalai.

clues.clue: The dearest cowherd friend Subala who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as this beloved devotee
The youthful cowherd boy known as Mahabala who took his eternal role in Sri Caitanya's sankirtana movement

129.verse_text: The devotee who had been the cowherd boy Subahu in Vrajabhumi appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Uddharana Datta. Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Mahabahu appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sriman Mahesa Pandita.

clues.clue: The personality who was known as the cowherd boy Subahu in Vrajabhumi during Krishna-lila
The associate of Lord Caitanya who previously acted as the cowherd friend Mahabahu in Vraja

130.verse_text: Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Stokakrsna appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Purusottama das.

clues.clue: The dear cowherd friend of Krishna who descended into the pastimes of Gauranga as Purusottama das
The fortunate devotee in the pastimes of Caitanya Mahaprabhu who was formerly the cowherd boy Stokakrsna

131.verse_text: Dama-gopa, who had been Lord Krsna's cowherd friend in Vraja, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Nagara Purusottama. Nagara Purusottama was born in a family of phyisicians, and his father's name was Sadasiva.

clues.clue: The dear cowherd friend of Lord Krsna in Vraja who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya
The father of the physician Nagara Purusottama who was born in a family of healers

132.verse_text: Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Arjuna appeared as Sri Paramesvara dasa in Lord Caitanya's pastimes. Lavanga, who had been Lord Krsna's cowherd friend in Vrajabhumi, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Kalakrsna dasa.

clues.clue: The Vrajabhumi cowherd friend of Lord Krsna who appeared in the pastimes of Sri Caitanya as Paramesvara dasa
The manifestation of the cowherd boy Arjuna who joined the transcendental pastimes of the golden Lord Caitanya
The cowherd companion of Krsna in Vrajabhumi who took the form of Sri Kalakrsna dasa in Gaura lila
The personality known in Vraja as Lavanga who entered the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as a humble servant

133.verse_text: The cowherd boy named Kusumasava, who joked with Lord Krsna and made Him laugh in Vrajabhumi, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the learned brahmana named Kholaveca Sridhara.

clues.clue: The cowherd boy in Vrajabhumi whose playful jokes brought laughter to the lotus lips of Lord Krsna
The learned brahmana who served the Lord and represents the eternal identity of the playful cowherd boy

134.verse_text: The cowherd boy Prabala, who was a close friend of Lord Balarama in Vrajabhumi, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Halayudha Thakura.

clues.clue: The cowherd boy of Vrajabhumi who enjoyed close friendship with Lord Balarama before appearing in Gauras pastimes
The eternal associate who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as the earthly manifestation of Prabala

135.verse_text: The cowherd boy Varuthapa who was close friend of Lord Krsnacandra in Vrajabhumi, appeared as Rudra Pandita, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The cowherd boy friend of Krsnacandra in Vrajabhumi who appeared as the dear associate Rudra Pandita
The dear devotee of Lord Caitanya who previously appeared as the cowherd boy Varuthapa in Vrajabhumi

136.verse_text: The devotee who had been the cowherd boy Gandharva in Vrajabhumi appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Kumudananda Pandita.

clues.clue: The youthful cowherd friend of Krsna in Vrajabhumi who later appeared as a pandita in Mahaprabhu's lila
The identity assumed in Lord Caitanya's pastimes by the cowherd boy who was previously known as Gandharva

137.verse_text: Bhrngara and Bhangura, who had been Lord Krsna's servants in Vrndavana, later appeared as Sri Kasisvara and Sri Govinda, the servants of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The devoted servant of Lord Caitanya who previously served as the cowherd boy Bhrngara in Vrndavana
The faithful attendant of Lord Caitanya who eternally served in Vraja as the cowherd boy Bhangura

138.verse_text: Raktaka and Patraka, who had been Lord Krsna's servants in Vrndavana, later appeared as Haridasa and Brhacchisu, two servants of Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The Vrajavasi servant of Krsna who descended to earth to become the associate known as Haridasa
The Vrajavasi servant of Krsna who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as Brhacchisu
The servant of Lord Caitanya who formerly served Krsna in Vrndavana as the personality named Raktaka
The associate of Lord Caitanya who held the eternal identity of Patraka in the forests of Vraja

139.verse_text: The two cowherdboys Payoda and Varida, who had formerly performed the nira-samskara ceremnoy for Lord Krsna, later appeared as Ramai and Nandai, two famous servants of Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The cowherd boy in Vrndavana who performed the nira-samskara ceremony for Lord Krsna and later became Ramai
The cowherd boy in Vrndavana who performed the nira-samskara ceremony for Lord Krsna and later became Nandai
The famous servant of Lord Caitanya who previously appeared in Vraja as the cowherd boy Payoda
The famous servant of Lord Caitanya who previously appeared in Vraja as the cowherd boy Varida

140.verse_text: Madhukantha and Madhuvrata, who were singers in Vrajabhumi, later appeared as Mukunda Datta and Vasudeva Datta, two famous singers in Lord Caitanya's kirtana party.

clues.clue: The singer in Lord Caitanya's kirtana party who previously served as Madhukantha in the pastures of Vrajabhumi.
The mellifluous singer in the Gaura-lila party who appeared as the manifestation of the Vraja singer Madhuvrata.

141.verse_text: Candramukha, the famous dancer in Vrndavana, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Makaradhvajakara.

clues.clue: The famous dancer of Vrndavana who took his earthly appearance in Lord Caitanya's lila to display devotional dance
The identity of the expert dancer in the eternal pastimes of Vrndavana before appearing as a Gaura associate

142.verse_text: Sri Sudhakara, who had been an expert mrdanga player in Vrajabhumi during Lord Krsna's pastimes, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the expert drummer Sri Sankara Ghosa.

clues.clue: The expert mrdanga player in Vrajabhumi who appeared as the drummer Sankara Ghosa in Gauras pastimes
The expert drummer in Lord Caitanyas pastimes who previously played the mrdanga during Lord Krsnas Vraja pastimes

143.verse_text: Candrahasa, who in Vrajabhumi had been a dancer expert in transcendental mellows, appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the dancer Jagadisa Pandita.

clues.clue: The dancer in Lord Caitanya's pastimes who previously performed with expertise in the mellows of Vrajabhumi
The expert dancer of Vrajabhumi who appeared as a prominent devotee in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya

144.verse_text: Maladhara, who had formerly carried Lord Krsna's venu and murali flutes in Vrajabhumi, later appeared as Vanamala Pandita, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The personality who carried the flute of Krishna in Vraja and became a devotee of Lord Caitanya
The dear associate of Lord Caitanya who previously served by carrying the flutes in Vrajabhumi

145.verse_text: Daksa and Vicaksana, the two famous parrots of Vrndavana, took birth during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Caitanya dasa and Rama dasa, who are both my seniors.

clues.clue: One of the two famous parrots of Vrndavana who took birth as a senior devotee of Lord Caitanya
The companion of Caitanya dasa who was previously known as the parrot Vicaksana in the pastimes of Vrndavana

146.verse_text: As much as I have heard from authorities, and as much as I can understand, I shall now describe Lord Krsna's dear friends, the gopis, and how they descended to participate in Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu's pastimes.

clues.clue: The golden savior who descended from Vraja to inaugurate the sankirtana movement in the mood of separation.

147.verse_text: Srimati Radharani, who is the personification of pure love for Lord Krsna and who is the queen of Vrndavana, appeared as Sri Gadadhara Pandita, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The dear associate of Lord Caitanya who is the personification of pure love and queen of Vrndavana
The transcendental forest realm where Srimati Radharani reigns as the queen of all divine love

148-150.verse_text: Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has also confirmed that the goddess of fortune, who appeared in Vrndavana and was very dear to Lord Krsna, has now appeared as Sri Gadadhara Pandita, who is filled with love for Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Lalita-gopi always follows Srimati Radharani, and therefore she is known as Anuradha (Radha's follower). When Srimati Radharani appeared as Gadadhara Pandita, Lalita followed her, entering the body of Gadadhara Pandita. Therefore, Gadadhara Pandita is the incarnation of both Srimati Radharani and Lalita-gopi. This is described in the following statement of Caitanya-candrodaya-nataka.

clues.clue: The dear associate who serves as the combined incarnation of Srimati Radharani and the gopi Lalita
The name by which Lalita is known because she always follows the footsteps of Srimati Radharani

151.verse_text: 'Gadadhara, the best of the brahmanas, is the incarnation of both Srimati Radharani and Lalita-gopi. These two are the transcendental potencies of the Lord, and since the Lord is in one sense not different from His potencies, it may be said that He is present wherever His potencies go. For this reason it may be said that Lord Hari is also present in the body of Gadadhara Pandita. Gadadhara is therefore the incarnation of three persons: Srimati Radharani, Lalita-gopi, and Lord Hari.'

clues.clue: The best of the brahmanas who is eternally the combined incarnation of Radharani and Lalita gopi
The intimate gopi who manifested alongside Srimati Radharani within the transcendental body of Gadadhara Pandita
The supreme Lord whose divine presence also resides within the sanctified body of Gadadhara Pandita

152.verse_text: Some say that Dhruvananda Brahmacari is the incarnation of Lalita-gopi. This opinion is also correct, for Lalita-gopi appeared as both Gadadhara Pandita and Dhruvananda Brahmacari.

clues.clue: The specific confidential associate of Srimati Radharani who manifested in both Gadadhara Pandita and Dhruvananda Brahmacari

153.verse_text: By His own will Lord Caitanya appeared in this world in three different forms at the same time. In the same way Srimati Radharani appeared as Gadadhara Pandita.

clues.clue: The intimate expansion of Srimati Radharani who appeared in Gaura-lila to serve the golden Lord

154.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's expansion as Candrakanti-devi appeared as Gadadhara dasa, a close associate of Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The intimate associate of Lord Caitanya who embodies the expansions of both Candrakanti-devi and the gopi Purnananda
The specific expansion of Srimati Radharani who manifest within the divine form of Gadadhara dasa

155.verse_text: Purnananda-gopi, the dearmost girl-friend of Lord Balarama also entered the body of Gadadhara dasa. Therefore Gadadhara dasa was the incarnation of both Candrakanti-devi and Purnananda-gopi.

clues.clue: The dearmost girlfriend of Lord Balarama who entered the transcendental body of Gadadhara dasa
The associate of Lord Caitanya who served as the dual incarnation of Candrakanti and Purnananda

156.verse_text: Candravali-gopi, who had been very dear to Lord Krsna in Vrajabhumi, appeared in Bengal as Sadasiva Kaviraja.

clues.clue: The gopi who was very dear to Lord Krsna in Vrajabhumi and appeared as Sadasiva Kaviraja
The devotee who appeared in Bengal as the earthly manifestation of the gopi named Candravali

157.verse_text: During Lord Krsna's Vrndavana pastimes He would sometimes sleep peacefully on the chest of Sribhadra-gopi. This same Sribhadra-gopi later appeared as Sankara Pandita, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The gopi upon whose chest the Lord would rest peacefully during His eternal pastimes in Vrndavana
The dear associate of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu who is the earthly manifestation of the gopi Sribhadra

158.verse_text: The vraja-gopis Taraka-devi and Pali-devi appeared as Sri Jagannatha and Sri Gopala, who were both very dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The Vraja gopi who took the form of Sri Jagannatha, a dear associate of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.
The Vraja gopi who manifested in Gaura lila as Sri Gopala, beloved by Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

159.verse_text: Hot-tempered Saibya-gopi who previously appeared in Vraja, appeared as Damodara Pandita during Lord Caitanya's pastimes. The goddess Sarasvati also entered the body of Damodara Pandita, who is thus the combined incarnation of them both.

clues.clue: The hot-tempered gopi from Vraja who manifested as the learned Damodara Pandita in Caitanya Mahaprabhu's pastimes
The combined incarnation of a Vraja gopi and the goddess of learning who served Lord Caitanya's pastimes

160.verse_text: Srimati Visakha-gopi, who formerly taught the various fine arts to Srimati Radharani in Vrajabhumi, appeared during Lord Caitanya's advent as Svarupa Damodara Gosvami, who was filled with the various splendid manifestations of ecstatic love of Godhead.

clues.clue: The Vrajabhumi gopi who formerly taught all the various fine arts to Srimati Radharani in Vraja

161.verse_text: Citra-devi, who had formerly combed and decorated Srimati Radharani's hair in Vrajabhumi, appeared as Sri Vanamali, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: The gopi who formerly arranged and decorated the beautiful hair of Srimati Radharani in the groves of Vrajabhumi
The associate dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu who is the eternal manifestation of the gopi Citra devi

162.verse_text: Srimati Campakalata-gopi, who considered Srimati Radharani as dear as her own life-breath, appeared as Raghava Gosvami, who lived at Govardhana Hill and wrote the book entitled Bhakti-ratna-prakasa.

clues.clue: The Vraja gopi who cherished Srimati Radharani as her own life breath and appeared in Gaura lila
The associate of Mahaprabhu who lived at Govardhana Hill and authored the text Bhakti ratna prakasa

163.verse_text: Tungavidya-gopi, who lived in Vrajabhumi and was learned in all the scriptures, appeared later as Prabodhananda Sarasvati, the poet who eloquently glorified the transcendental qualities of Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The learned gopi of Vrajabhumi who later manifested as the poet who glorified the qualities of Lord Caitanya

164.verse_text: Indulekha-gopi, who was Srimati Radharani's close friend in Vrajabhumi, appeared as Krsnadasa Brahmacari, who resided in Vrndavana-dhama.

clues.clue: The intimate friend of Srimati Radharani in Vrajabhumi who appeared as Krsnadasa Brahmacari in Gaura-lila

165.verse_text: The vraja-gopi Rangadevi appeared as Gadadhara Bhatta, and the vraja-gopi Sudevi appeared as Ananta Acarya Gosvami.

clues.clue: The vraja-gopi Rangadevi manifested her eternal service to Lord Gauranga in the form of this learned devotee.

166.verse_text: The vraja-gopi Sasirekha-devi appeared as Sri Kasisvara Gosvami. The vraja-gopi Dhanistha-devi, who had previously fed Lord Krsna with limitless opulent foodstuffs, appeared as Raghava Pandita, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The Vraja gopi Sasirekha-devi manifested her eternal devotion in this form to serve Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu
The personality who formerly fed Krsna opulent foodstuffs and later became extremely dear to Lord Caitanya

167.verse_text: The vraja-gopi Gunamala-devi appeared as Raghava Pandita's sister Damayanti-devi. Ratnarekha-gopi appeared as Krsnadasa, and Kalavati-gopi appeared as Krsnananda.

clues.clue: The sister of Raghava Pandita who is the eternal manifestation of the Vraja-gopi known as Gunamala-devi
The personality who appeared in Gaura-lila as the eternal expansion of the Vraja-gopi named Ratnarekha
The devotee who revealed his identity as the manifestation of the Vraja-gopi famously known as Kalavati

168.verse_text: Gauraseni-gopi appeared as the saintly devotee Narayana Vacaspati. Kaveri-gopi appeared as Pitambara, and Sukesi-gopi appeared as Makaradhvaja.

clues.clue: The saintly devotee who manifested in Gaura-lila as the eternal expansion of the gopi known as Gauraseni
The eternal associate of Sri Caitanya who descended to this world as the expansion of Kaveri-gopi
The personality appearing in the pastimes of the golden Lord who is the manifestation of Sukesi-gopi

169.verse_text: Madhavi-gopi appeared as Madhvacarya, and Indira-gopi appeared as Jiva Pandita.

clues.clue: The ancient sampradaya acharya who is identified as the transcendental manifestation of the Vraja-gopi named Madhavi
The learned associate of Lord Caitanya who eternally serves as the earthly form of the gopi Indira

170.verse_text: The charming vraja-gopi named Tungavidya appeared as Vidyavacaspati, who was dear to both Lord Caitanya and the residents of Vrndavana.

clues.clue: The charming vraja-gopi who appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as the scholar Vidyavacaspati
The dear associate of Lord Caitanya and the residents of Vrndavana who was previously the gopi Tungavidya

171.verse_text: Madhureksana-gopi appeared as Balabhadra Bhattacarya, Citrangi-gopi appeared as Srinatha Misra, and Manohara-gopi appeared as Kavicandra.

clues.clue: The personality appearing in Gaura lila as the manifestation of the Vraja gopi named Madhureksana
The devoted soul identified by Kavi Karnapura as the earthly appearance of the gopi named Citrangi
The name of the individual who appeared in this world as the expansion of Manohara gopi

172.verse_text: The vraja-brahmani Nandimukhi-devi appeared as Saranga Thakura. Some people think that Saranga Thakura was the incarnation of Prahlada Maharaja. My father (Sivananda Sena) did not agree with them.

clues.clue: The Vraja brahmani from the eternal pastimes who took the earthly form of Saranga Thakura
The personality some mistakenly identified as Prahlada Maharaja, though Sivananda Sena held a different view
The author's father who respectfully disagreed with those claiming Saranga Thakura was Prahlada Maharaja

173.verse_text: Kalakantha-devi and Sukantha-devi, the two Gandharva-dancers who appeared in Vrajabhumi, also appeared as Ramananda Vasu and Satyaraja.

clues.clue: A Gandharva-dancer from the eternal Vrajabhumi who descended to play the role of Ramananda Vasu
A divine Gandharva-dancer who appeared in this world to take on the identity of Satyaraja
The personality who manifested in Gaura-lila as the earthly expansion of the Gandharva-dancer Kalakantha-devi
The devoted soul who carried the identity of the Gandharva-dancer Sukantha-devi within the pastimes of Gauranga

174.verse_text: The vraja-gopi Katyayani-devi appeared as Srikanta Sena.

clues.clue: The specific Vraja gopi who took birth in the pastimes of Gaura as Srikanta Sena
The personality who manifested in Gaura-lila as the eternal expansion of the Vraja gopi Katyayani-devi

175.verse_text: Srimati Vrnda-devi, the presiding deity of Vrajabhumi, appeared as Sri Mukunda dasa, who lived in the village of Srikhanda and was very dear to the Lord.

clues.clue: The presiding goddess of the sacred groves of Vrajabhumi who appeared in Gaura-lila as Sri Mukunda dasa
The dear devotee residing in Srikhanda who is the eternal manifestation of the presiding deity of Vrajabhumi
The village home of the devoted soul who serves as the manifestation of Srimati Vrnda-devi

176.verse_text: The two gopis Vira-devi and Duti-devi, who acted as messengers arranging the gopis' meetings with Lord Krsna, combined to appear as my father (Sivananda Sena). The vraja-gopi Bindumati appeared as my mother.

clues.clue: The father of the author Kavi Karnapura who appeared as the messenger gopis Vira and Duti
The vraja gopi who took the role of the mother of the author Kavi Karnapura

177.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's dear friend, the vraja-gopi named Madhumati-devi, appeared as Narahari Sarakara, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The vraja-gopi and dear friend of Srimati Radharani who descended into Gaura-lila as Narahari Sarakara
The intimate associate very dear to Lord Caitanya who manifested from the vraja-gopi known as Madhumati

178.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's dear friend, the vraja-gopi named Ratnavali-devi appeared as Gopinatha Acarya, who was famous for his purity.

clues.clue: The dear friend of Srimati Radharani who descended into the pastimes of Gaura as Gopinatha Acarya
The saintly personality famous for his purity who was the earthly manifestation of the gopi Ratnavali

179.verse_text: Lord Krsna's favorite flute appeared as Vamsidasa Thakura.

clues.clue: The eternal and blissful flute of Lord Krsna who appeared in Gaura lila as a devotee

213.verse_text: I have written this book in such a way that the Mimamsakas, false logicians, and others who relish dry philosophical debate will not be able to understand it, even with the greatest endeavor. Only the devotees, who are learned in the rasa-sastras, and who continually take shelter of Lord Caitanya's lotus feet, will be able to understand this book properly. I have written this book for them.

clues.clue: The golden Lord whose lotus feet are the constant, singular shelter for those who relish the rasa-sastras.
