Those who relish dry philosophical debate and lack the capacity to understand the confidential truths of this book.

180.verse_text: Sri Rupa-manjari, who had been famous in Vrndavana-dhama, appeared as Srila Rupa Gosvami.

clues.clue: The manifestation of the beautiful Rupa Manjari who graced the holy land of Vrndavana before appearing in Gaura-lila.

181.verse_text: Rupa-manjari's closest friend, who was known by the names Rati-manjari and Lavanga-manjari, appeared as Srila Sanatana Gosvami, who was honored by everyone, and who was considered to be like an extension of the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Sanatana Kumara, the jewel among the sages, entered the body of Sanatana Gosvami, who is therefore also considered to be an incarnation of Sanatana Kumara.

clues.clue: The personality honored by all and considered a direct extension of the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya
The identity of the close friend of Rupa Manjari who manifested as a jewel among the sages

182.verse_text: Sivananda Cakravarti, who lived in Vrndavana, is also considered to be an incarnation of Srimati Lavanga-manjari.

clues.clue: The resident of Vrndavana who is celebrated as an incarnation of the gopi named Lavangamanjari
The gopi whose transcendental identity manifested in this world as the venerable Sivananda Cakravarti

184.verse_text: Ananga-manjari appeared as Srila Gopala Bhatta Gosvami. Some say that Gopala Bhatta Gosvami was actually the incarnation of Sri Guna-manjari.

clues.clue: The Vraja-gopi who appeared in the pastimes of Gaura-lila as the esteemed Srila Gopala Bhatta Gosvami
The saintly associate of Lord Caitanya identified as the manifestation of Ananga-manjari or perhaps Guna-manjari
The eternal companion of Srimati Radharani who is alternatively considered the source of Gopala Bhatta Gosvami

185.verse_text: Raga-manjari appeared as Srila Raghunatha bhatta Gosvami, who lived in a cottage by the shore of Radha-kunda.

clues.clue: The eternal expansion of Raga-manjari who performed his loving service within a cottage by Radha-kunda
The sacred lake on the banks of which the manifestation of Raga-manjari built his simple cottage

186.verse_text: Srila Raghunatha dasa Gosvami was the incarnation of Rasa-manjari. Some say that Raghunatha das Gosvami was the incarnation of Rati-manjari, and others say he was the incarnation of Bhanumati-devi.

clues.clue: The exalted follower of the Gosvamis who is glorified as the incarnation of Rasa-manjari or Rati-manjari
The eternal personality in the spiritual world whom some scholars identify as the incarnation of Raghunatha-dasa

187.verse_text: Bhugarbha Thakura was the incarnation of Prema-manjari. Lokanatha Gosvami was the incarnation of Lila-manjari.

clues.clue: The devoted follower who appeared in Gaura lila as the eternal manifestation of the gopi named Prema manjari
The intimate associate of Mahaprabhu who is eternally known as the manifestation of the gopi Lila manjari

188.verse_text: The vraja-gopis Kalavati-devi, Rasollasa-devi, and Gunatunga-devi, who sang the songs composed by Visakha-devi, later appeared as Govinda, Madhavananda, and Vasudeva respectively.

clues.clue: The Vraja-gopi and singer of Visakha-devi's compositions who appeared in Gaura-lila as the devotee known as Govinda
The musical Vraja-gopi who sang the songs of Visakha-devi and later appeared in Gaura-lila as Madhavananda
The Vraja-gopi singer of Visakha-devi's songs who took the identity of Vasudeva in the pastimes of the Lord

189.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's maidservants, Rangalekha-devi and Kalakeli-devi, appeared as Sikhi-mahiti and his sister, Madhavi-devi respectively.

clues.clue: The devoted attendant of Srimati Radharani named Rangalekha who manifested as this male associate of Lord Caitanya
The maidservant of the Divine Queen Radharani known as Kalakeli who appeared in Gaura-lila as this sister

190.verse_text: Malli-devi, the daughter of a Pulinda, appeared as Kalidasa.

clues.clue: The devotee who is the manifestation of the tribal daughter Mallidevi within the pastimes of Lord Caitanya

191.verse_text: Suklambara Brahmacari was the incarnation of one of the yajna-patnis who gave food in charity to Krsna and his friends. Lord Caitanya was accustomed to eating food He had begged from Suklambara Brahmacari. Some say that Suklambara Brahmacari was not one of the yajna-patnis, but one of their husbands, the yajnika-brahmanas.

clues.clue: The devoted associate who provided food to Lord Caitanya that He habitually begged from him.

192.verse_text: Two other yajna-patnis appeared as Jagadisa and Hiranya. One ekadasi day Lord Caitanya ate grains he had begged from them.

clues.clue: One of the two yajna patnis who appeared in Gaura lila to provide grains to Lord Caitanya
A yajna patni who appeared alongside Jagadisa to offer grains to the golden Lord during Ekadasi

193.verse_text: Kubja, who had been very to dear to Lord Krsna in Mathura, later appeared in Jagannatha Puri as Kasi Misra, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

clues.clue: The resident of Jagannatha Puri who appeared as the manifestation of the Mathura resident beloved by Krsna
The lady of Mathura who was very dear to Lord Krsna and appeared as a devotee in Puri

194-207.verse_text: Malati, Candralatika, Manjumedha, Varangada, Ratnavali, Kamala, Gunacuda, Sukesini, Karpura-manjari, Syama-manjari, Sveta-manjari, Vilasa-manjari, Kamalekha, Mauna-manjari, Gandhonmada, Rasonmada, Candrika, and Candrasekhara, were among the maidservants of Srimati Radharani. Assuming male forms they appeared among Lord Caitanya's associates, and each displayed his particular kind of ecstatic love for the Lord. These gopis became Subhananda Dvija, Sridhara Brahmacari, Paramananda Gupta (who wrote the book Sri Krsna-stavavali), Raghunatha Dvija (who was an unalloyed servant of Lord Caitanya), Kamsari Sena, Sri Jagannatha Sena Mahasaya, Subuddhi Misra, Sriharsa, Raghu Misra (who was the best of the brahmanas), Jitamitra (whose appropriate name, meaning 'conqueror of the enemies', was given by Lord Caitanya because Jitamitra had completely subdued lust and the other five enemies of the conditioned soul), Sriman Bhagavatacarya (who was very dear to Lord Caitanya, and who wrote the book Sri Krsna-prema-tarangini), Sriman Jiva Pandita (who was the saintly son of Sri Vallabha), Vaninatha Dvija (who lived in the village of Campahatta, and who was very dear to Lord Caitanya), Isanacarya, Kamala, Laksminatha Pandita, Ganga-mantri, Jagannatha Mamu (who was a very elevated brahmana), Srikanthabharana Ananta (who was born in the Catta dynasty), Hastigopala (who lived in Rangaksetra, and who was very dear to Lord Caitanya), Hari Acarya, Srinayana Misra (who was a close associate of Lord Caitanya), Kavidatta, Ramadasa, Ciranjiva, and Sulocana respectively.

clues.clue: The saintly associate dear to Lord Caitanya who authored the literary work known as Sri Krsna Prema Tarangini
The personality given a name by Lord Caitanya signifying he completely conquered the enemies of the conditioned soul
The resident of Rangaksetra who was exceedingly dear to Lord Caitanya among the list of Srimati Radharanis maidservants

208.verse_text: Someone may consider that among these devotees some are greatly exalted and others are a little less. I will not make any such distinctions. For me all these great souls are equal.

clues.clue: The humble author who considers all the great souls equal and avoids making any personal distinctions

209.verse_text: Ciranjiva and Sulocana lived in the village of Srihatta. They were completely surrendered to Lord Caitanya, and because they were the friends and helpers of Narahari dasa they were honored and considered great souls.

clues.clue: One of the two brothers who resided in Srihatta and remained completely surrendered to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu
The faithful brother of Ciranjiva who served as a helpful companion to the exalted devotee Narahari dasa
The exalted devotee whose friendships and spiritual activities were supported by the brothers Ciranjiva and Sulocana

210.verse_text: The scriptures state: 'A disciple should not address his spiritual master by name'. For this reason I have not mentioned the previous name of my spiritual master, Sri Srinatha Cakravarti.

clues.clue: The spiritual master of the author Kavi Karnapura whose name is respectfully omitted from direct address

211.verse_text: All glories to Sri Krsnadeva of Kumara-hatta, the author of the commentary on Sri Bhagavata-samhita. His fame is always splendidly manifest.

clues.clue: The glorious author of the Bhagavata-samhita commentary who resides in the holy village of Kumara-hatta

212.verse_text: If I have made any errors in describing the great souls mentioned in this book, I beg those great personalities to please forgive me. Neither Lord Brahma, Lord Siva, nor Lord Ananta Sesa are able to describe completely the exalted virtues of those great souls.

clues.clue: The creator deity of the material universe who remains unable to fully describe the virtues of these great souls.
The thousand headed serpent manifestation of the Lord who finds it impossible to fully glorify these exalted devotees.

214.verse_text: I pray that this book I have written, the Sri Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika (a lamp to see the associates of Lord Caitanya), may shine in the homes of the blissful devotees of the Lord.

clues.clue: The lamp that reveals the associates of Lord Caitanya composed by the poet Sri Kavi Karnapura
The golden Lord whose associates are illuminated by the light of this devotional literary composition

215.verse_text: This book was written in the year 1498 of the Saka era (corresponding to 1576 A.D.). I pray that those great devotees whose minds are immersed in the nectar of Lord Caitanyacandra's pastimes may read this book critically and correct all its faults.

clues.clue: The golden Lord whose pastimes are the sweet nectar in which the minds of the devotees immerse
